description:
Create a four-file Talon voice command system for forensic chain-of-custody logging in C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\evidence_logger\.

File 1: `evidence_logger.talon`
Enable the tag `user.evidence_logging` and implement the following voice commands to call corresponding actions: 'evidence new case <user.text>', 'evidence log item <user.text>', 'evidence handler <user.text>', 'evidence action {user.evidence_action}', 'evidence location <user.text>', 'evidence commit entry', 'evidence show log', 'evidence export csv', and 'evidence clear'.

File 2: `evidence_actions.talon-list`
Define the list `user.evidence_action` containing exactly these ten actions: received, transferred, stored, retrieved, examined, photographed, returned, disposed, sealed, and unsealed.

File 3: `evidence_logger.py`
Implement the Python actions for the voice commands. The system must log data to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\chain_of_custody.csv with the headers: CaseID, Timestamp, EvidenceItem, Handler, Action, Location, EntryNumber. The commit logic must auto-generate ISO 8601 timestamps and auto-incrementing EntryNumbers.

File 4: `evidence_mode.talon`
Enable the `user.evidence_logging` tag when the active window title contains 'Evidence Logger'. Include a global command 'evidence mode on' that opens the Evidence Logger interface.
